---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2024-10-21"
---

### This script is used for constructing Figure 1. 

```{r}

library(ggplot2)

library(dplyr)

library(readr)

```


```{r}

df <- read.csv('buttress_plot_pixel_aggregate_20251219.csv')


```


```{r}
t1<-df %>%
  ggplot(., aes(x = abs(pixellat), y = meanfraction))+
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1)+
  geom_smooth(method = 'glm', method.args = list(family = 'binomial'))+
  labs(x = 'Latitude', y = 'Buttress root fraction (%)')+
  #coord_flip()+
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'sqrt')+
    theme_classic()+
    theme(axis.text = element_text(color = '#000000', size = 20), axis.title = element_text(color = '#000000', size = 20))


```



```{r}
library(maps)
library(tidyverse)
worlddata <- map_data('world') 
library(sp)

library(broom)
library(raster)
library(mapproj)

library(viridis)

###wwf <- shapefile('buttress_root\\biome1_wwf.shp')

  t2<-ggplot() + 
  geom_map(data = worlddata, map = worlddata, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group, map_id = region), fill = '#8d99ae', color = '#8d99ae', size = 0.5)+
    geom_polygon(data = wwf, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), fill = '#8ecae6')+
    #coord_sf(crs = 8857, default_crs = 4326)+
  geom_point(data = df, aes(x = pixellon, y = pixellat, color = fraction), size = 1)+
  labs(x = 'Longitude', y = 'Latitude')+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_viridis_c(limits = c(0,0.5), oob = scales::squish, direction = -1)+
  #scale_color_manual(values = c('#3a8c2d'))+
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(), axis.line = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank(), legend.position = "none", axis.text = element_blank())
  #t2<-ggExtra::ggMarginal(t2,type = 'histogram', margins = c('y'))


```


```{r}
library(ggpubr)

library(cowplot)

ggarrange(t1,t2,
          #t3,
          ncol = 1,
          nrow = 2,
          #widths = c(0.8,1,0.8),
          heights = c(1,2))


  #draw_plot_label(label = c("A", "B"), size = 15,
                  #x = c(0, 0.5, 0), y = c(1, 1, 0.5))
```
